page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.r7t.large is a memory-optimized virtual server instance running on the KVM hypervisor. It features 2 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 2 physical cores of an Intel Xeon 8369B processor operating at 2.7 GHz. The system is equipped with 16.0 GB of RAM, yielding a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per core. The underlying x86_64 architecture supports advanced instruction sets including AVX, AVX-512 (specifically AVX512F and AVX512_VNNI), and cryptographic acceleration via AES and SHA_NI. This instance does not include local storage or GPU hardware, and it provides a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps.

Benchmark evaluations reveal a performance profile characterized by average single-core capabilities but weak multi-core and memory throughput. The single-core performance is demonstrated by a stress-ng score of 1873.79 and a Geekbench single-core score of 1580.0, both falling within the average range. However, multi-core scaling is limited, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 1935.57 and a Geekbench multi-core score of 1823.0, both categorized as poor. Memory bandwidth tests also show constraints, with uncached read and write speeds of 13.97 GB/sec and 12.92 GB/sec respectively.

Given the high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 1:8, the ecs.r7t.large is qualitatively structured for memory-intensive workloads that do not require high parallel processing power. It is suitable for lightweight database operations, caching layers, and text processing tasks. However, its poor multi-core performance and weak database operation benchmarks make it less suitable for high-throughput transactional databases or heavy compilation tasks. The instance offers a dedicated CPU allocation, providing predictable single-threaded execution for budget-conscious deployments requiring a larger memory footprint relative to compute resources.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.r7t.large is a memory-optimized virtual server powered by the KVM hypervisor and an Intel Xeon 8369B processor. It features 2 dedicated vCPUs (2 physical cores) running at 2.7 GHz, paired with 16.0 GB of RAM, which provides a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per core. The x86_64 architecture supports AVX-512 and cryptographic instructions, though the instance lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. Performance benchmarks indicate average single-core execution, with a Geekbench single-core score of 1580.0, but poor multi-core scaling and constrained memory bandwidth. With a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps, this instance is qualitatively cost-efficient for memory-sensitive, low-concurrency workloads. It is best suited for lightweight caching, text processing, and basic database tasks that benefit from dedicated CPU allocation and a larger memory footprint without requiring intensive parallel computation.
og_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.r7t.large is a memory-optimized KVM instance featuring 2 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 16.0 GB of RAM, and a 2 Gbps network baseline, suitable for lightweight caching and memory-sensitive workloads.

tagline: A memory-optimized virtual server featuring dedicated Intel Xeon cores and a high memory-to-vCPU ratio for lightweight, memory-sensitive workloads.
